[[BLOCK:p_68]]
Technical Statement:

[[BLOCK:p_69]]
Although Hawaii Pacific University (HPU) provides the educational software Lumina for classroom use, you are responsible for ensuring that you have reliable access to a computer with an internet connection. In addition to a web browser, your computer must have Microsoft Word, PowerPoint, Excel, Adobe Reader, and Media Player installed. If you experience any technical difficulties, please contact the Beacon staff.

[[BLOCK:p_70]]
Academic Integrity Statement:

[[BLOCK:p_71]]
At Hawaii Pacific University (HPU), students are expected to conduct themselves with respect, honor, and integrity. When submitting academic work, students must comply with the university’s academic integrity policy. This website is available to help students understand the policy and avoid acts of academic dishonesty.

[[BLOCK:p_72]]
HPU’s academic integrity policy states that any act of academic dishonesty will result in disciplinary action, up to and including dismissal from the university. Students who cheat on academic assignments, provide unauthorized assistance to others, or submit work that is not their own will be subject to penalties. “Academic assignments” include all forms of work submitted for credit, whether electronically or in print.

[[BLOCK:p_73]]
For detailed definitions of academic integrity violations and the procedures for addressing them, please refer to the HPU Student Handbook.

[[BLOCK:p_75]]
Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) (ADA) Statement: Pursuant to Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 (《1973 年康复法案》（第 504 节）) and Title III of the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) (ADA) (Public Accommodations).

[[BLOCK:p_76]]
Hawaii Pacific University (HPU) will make reasonable modifications to its policies, practices, and procedures to ensure that: (1) students with disabilities have equal access to the services and facilities offered by the university; and (2) qualified individuals with disabilities are employed if they are able to perform the essential functions of the position. HPU will provide accommodations for known disabilities unless doing so would impose an undue hardship on the university. “Undue hardship” refers to significant difficulty or expense, including fundamental alterations to the nature of the university’s services and facilities.
